title: Public Identity, Privacy, Security, and Reputation Boundaries · Hiring Decision Lens: Data Inventory
summary: A learner is told to make everything public, revealing contact details, location patterns, secrets, client material, protected identity, private history, and unsafe availability. Predict the hiring decision, evidence quality, affected people, uncertainty, and ethical boundary before reviewing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the data inventory increment in public-identity threat and disclosure model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal d…
objectives: Inventory personal, third-party, employer, client, research, secret, metadata, and inferred information before publication.
Choose a public, selective, private, or live-only evidence channel from audience need, learner safety, and claim purpose.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Predict Data Inventory · Theory Data Inventory
steps.instruction:
theory case — A public profile leaks private email, precise location, commit identity, client screenshots, API history, availability, and a protected personal detail. The theory variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Inventory personal, third-party, employer, client, research, secret, metadata, and inferred information before publication.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Data Inventory contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Data Inventory · Theory Data Inventory.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inventory personal, third-party, employer, client, research, secret, metadata, and inferred information before publication. For Predict Data Inventory · Theory Data Inventory, keep Data Inventory connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A public profile leaks private email, precise location, commit identity, client screenshots, API history, availability, and a protected personal detail. The theory variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Inventory personal, third-party, employer, client, research, secret, metadata, and inferred information before publication.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Data Inventory condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: GitHub Profile Documentation — Controls profile visibility, optional public identity, pinned work, contribution context, and the personal safety consequences of public disclosure.

hints: For Predict Data Inventory · Theory Data Inventory, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Data Inventory precondition, or invariant that must hold.

The theory variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Inventory personal, third-party, employer, client, research, secret, metadata, and inferred information before publication.

For Predict Data Inventory · Theory Data Inventory, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: Deleting visible text removes all sensitive history and metadata.

options.text: Deleting visible text removes all sensitive history and metadata. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Data Inventory.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Inventory personal, third-party, employer, client, research, secret, metadata, and inferred information before publication. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

content.text: Inventory personal, third-party, employer, client, research, secret, metadata, and inferred information before publication. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, changed candidate, rejected failure, causal repair, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer limit; then calibrate the application or career decision to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A public profile leaks private email, precise location, commit identity, client screenshots, API history, availability, and a protected personal detail. The worked example variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Inventory personal, third-party, employer, client, research, secret, metadata, and inferred information before publication. A defensible solution applies Data Inventory, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to public-identity threat and disclosure model.

content.code:
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-candidate-2
target-role: accessible software quality engineer
jurisdiction: example location requiring current official review
audience: technical reviewer with ten minutes and keyboard-only access
claim: one bounded role behavior is supported by a revision-linked changed case
evidence: repository case study, exact test result, contribution note, and limitation
process-state: investigate before applying
accessibility: structured text, reflow, visible focus, plain-language status, and equivalent format
privacy-consent: publish no sensitive person or third-party data; contact only with explicit consent
decision: proceed only after identity, role, evidence, access, and risk checks pass
verify: inspect links, extraction order, revision, changed case, disclosure, and source date
failure: reject invented impact, hidden assistance, exposed data, or unverified recruiter identity
repair: correct the first unsupported claim and add a changed-candidate regression
owner: learner controls every external action
transfer-boundary: manual learner-approved profile, application, conversation, interview, and offer decisions
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Deleting visible text removes all sensitive history and metadata. This fails the Data Inventory contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: GitHub Profile Documentation (GitHub Docs current 2026-07-15) — Controls profile visibility, optional public identity, pinned work, contribution context, and the personal safety consequences of public disclosure.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inventory personal, third-party, employer, client, research, secret, metadata, and inferred information before publication.
